title: H4M WORKFORCE ACCOUNTABILITY ATTENDANCE AND PAYROLL
brief: Full-stack admin system for employee records, attendance monitoring, payroll workflows, reports, and Vercel deployment.
description: Built the frontend and backend for H4M WORKFORCE ACCOUNTABILITY ATTENDANCE AND PAYROLL, including employee records, attendance monitoring, payroll workflows, reports, and Vercel deployment.
fullDetails: Created the frontend admin panel with EJS and Tailwind CSS for dashboard, attendance, payroll, profile, invoice, announcements, and user management screens.
Built backend workflows with Node.js to support workforce records, monitoring pages, employee-related features, and admin panel navigation.
Improved the project weekly through feature enhancements, UI polish, device recognition work, invoice improvements, and final deployment preparation in Vercel.

title: Research Government Statutory Computations
brief: Research and documentation for government statutory payroll computations, covering required contribution and deduction concepts used in payroll processing.
description: A research package focused on government statutory computations for payroll, documenting how required employee deductions and employer contributions are identified, organized, and prepared for accurate payroll system implementation.
fullDetails: Reviewed the statutory computation requirements that payroll systems need to support, including government-mandated contribution and deduction workflows.
Organized the research into reference documents so the computation logic can be understood before being translated into system features.
Connected the findings to payroll accuracy, compliance preparation, employee record keeping, and clearer payroll reporting.

title: Workforce Accountability Research
brief: Research and planning phase for the attendance and payroll system, covering the problem, target user, empathy map, SCAMPER ideas, and proposed solution.
description: A research package for the Workforce Accountability and Attendance System that defines the LGU attendance problem, studies the HR officer user, maps pains and goals, and turns the findings into solution ideas for secure attendance verification, monitoring, and payroll support.
fullDetails: Defined the core problem: manual or outdated attendance workflows can create inaccurate records, proxy logging, payroll errors, delayed reporting, and scattered employee files.
Created a user persona for an HR officer responsible for monitoring attendance, preparing payroll reports, reducing computation errors, and keeping records audit-ready.
Built an empathy map to capture what the user hears, sees, thinks, feels, says, and does during daily attendance and payroll work.

Used SCAMPER and brainstorming to shape solution directions such as QR code and photo capture, GPS validation, real-time attendance monitoring, centralized records, role-based access, and payroll integration.

title: Practice Web
brief: Two-week Node.js learning project for practicing login, dashboard, employee logs, payroll, profile, and user management screens.
description: A two-week learning project using Node.js, EJS, and Tailwind CSS to practice backend and frontend workflows for login, dashboard, employee logs, payroll, profile, and user management screens.
fullDetails: Week 1 focused on setting up the Node.js project structure and practicing EJS rendering through login, dashboard, and employee logs screens.
Week 2 focused on payroll, profile, and user management screens while improving Tailwind CSS layout and admin interface consistency.
The practice project helped prepare the frontend and backend workflow used later in H4M WORKFORCE ACCOUNTABILITY ATTENDANCE AND PAYROLL.
